mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0% and remains elevated for two consecutive months, equity risk premiums expand as the real discount rate rises, compressing present values of future corporate earnings and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rates above neutral
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window when FEDFUNDS > 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following UNRATE increases > 0.3pp are positive in more than 50% of instances, or joint UNRATE-PAYEMS signal produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 2% year-over-year while housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1,400 thousand units, indicating weakening consumer demand and construction activity that foreshadows earnings downgrades and negative equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS YoY growth < 2% AND HOUST < 1400 thousand units simultaneously
regime_hint: demand-constrained environment with synchronized weakness across consumer and housing sectors
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 52% of co-triggered instances (both conditions met), or interaction Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) expands at a rate exceeding 0.5% month-over-month for two consecutive months, signaling robust supply-side capacity utilization and productivity gains that support corporate profit margins and generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM growth > 0.5% for 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: supply-driven expansion with improving capacity utilization
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following INDPRO acceleration > 0.5% MoM are negative in more than 48% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation (CPIAUCSL) decelerates by more than 0.2 index points month-over-month while the federal funds rate remains above 3.5%, indicating that monetary policy is successfully containing price pressures without further tightening, reducing tail risks and supporting equity valuations with positive 60-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL MoM decline > 0.2 points AND FEDFUNDS > 3.5%
regime_hint: disinflation phase within a restrictive monetary policy regime
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of co-triggered instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains above 2.5% annualized while housing starts (HOUST) exceed 1,500 thousand units, indicating sustained demand momentum and construction strength that supports earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as sentiment improves.
regime_hint: any regime with sustained above-trend growth
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of observations when both GDPC1 > 2.5% annualized and HOUST > 1500K, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100 thousand new jobs per month while the unemployment rate remains stable or rises, signaling labor market slack that constrains wage inflation and supports equity valuations through lower discount rates, producing positive 60-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM growth < 100K AND UNRATE stable or rising
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market softening with disinflationary implications
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 52% of co-triggered instances, or joint PAYEMS-UNRATE interaction Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) contracts by more than 0.3% month-over-month while real retail sales (RSAFS) simultaneously decline, indicating synchronized weakness across supply and demand that signals recession risk and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings expectations collapse.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM decline > 0.3% AND RSAFS MoM decline simultaneously
regime_hint: synchronized supply-demand contraction signaling recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 48% of co-triggered instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.
